title: lidia biflora
intro: Lidia biflora, also known as the two-flowered lidia, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lidia biflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Lidia biflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it may also be used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
